column18: My electricity bill stays between 300 and 700 dollars per month depending on weather. Heat and ACs are insanely expensive. I tried not to run ACs last year and me and some of my kids got heatstroke during a heatwave. It is constantly paid late. My vehicle is falling apart and needs a transmission, I have six kids(one is a disabled toddler with appointments constantly), I drive hundreds of miles every few days and spend thousands of dollars a year on gas, resorting to borrowing and begging for money, my oldest turned 18 and was removed from my foodstamps despite living in the home and starting college, getting enough food is a struggle and I go to food pantries often. I am grateful for help I have received, but the constant struggle is disheartening, I am in college full time so that I can have a better chance at escaping poverty and giving my kids a good role model. I'm the first in my family to attend any university for generations.

column18: I'm a single person renting a one bedroom apartment in Portland. On my teacher's salary, I struggle to afford to live here, and have accumulated some credit card debt. I feel like I'm always digging myself out with each paycheck, throwing as much money as I can toward it. I'm worried about adding student loan repayment to the mix. And if I have an unexpected bill, it just sets me back all over again. I've had to give up a hobby to put the money I would have spent on that toward paying down credit card debt. I have basically no savings and live paycheck to paycheck.

column18: I am a single homeowner, living in South Portland. I have lived there for over 14 years and over the past few years I have been struggling to make it. I work a full time job and another job on the weekend. After being diagnosed with metastatic breast cancer, I had to change my career path to one with less stress. Less stress=less money. Since then, everything has gone up. My mortgage, groceries and medical bills are big ones. Being able to access this program has been extremely helpful. Saving on having to pay to have my taxes done helps me get the most money back so I can try to pay a few things down. Thank you from the bottom of my heart.

column8: I was born raised and work within the communities of the lakes region and while I dont mind people from away moving to the area their good intentions are destroying the culture of Maine People move here for the beauty of the area wanting to be part of a community after living autonomously in cities Yet when they get here all they want to do is bring everything with them they left behind It has raised the cost of living eliminated neighbors not only knowing each other but is eradicating the inclusionary culture to care for one another which is creating social isolation for many Just going to throw out there we are not poor Mainers who need to be fixed we need to educate the people from away on the culture of traditional Maine We as a collective are not opposed to growth but it needs to happen in a way that is purposeful thoughtful and has longterm sustainability that actually addresses the actual needs of the communities we all live in I dont want to take another survey on the same topics areas that have needed improvement for 30 years I want to see solutions But I guess if transportation housing and food insecurity are all resolved then no one makes any money But wouldnt it be nice to focus on improving other issues like mental health substance abuse child and adult welfare systems stabilizing and improving schools  I could go on but there is no point I will still see another survey like this and the issues will remain the same unresolved but at least the question was asked right

column8: I work in Biddeford and feel like the voices of the nonwhite folks here are not being heard like they should be I also feel like people of an older age demographic tend to not take issues that affect the younger ages as seriously because its not what they experienced Things like buying a house or going to college used to be SIGNIFICANTLY less expensive so many tend to blow off concerns about those topics Yea in 1955 you bought your house for $30k and paid for your college tuition for $8k all while working at normal job and supporting a family Its not like that anymore
